title: Network Connectivity Repair
description: Diagnose and repair common Windows network connectivity issues with read-only checks before any reset.
starter_prompt: Diagnose this PC's network connectivity problem. Start read-only and ask before resets.

phases.prompt: Confirm symptom, affected network, wired/wireless, VPN/proxy use, and when it started.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Get-NetAdapter =='; Get-NetAdapter | Format-List Name,Status,InterfaceDescription,LinkSpeed,MacAddress; Write-Output '== Get-NetIPConfiguration =='; Get-NetIPConfiguration | Format-List; Write-Output '== DNS Servers =='; Get-DnsClientServerAddress | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Output '== ipconfig /all =='; ipconfig /all; Write-Output '== route print =='; route print; Write-Output '== Recent network events =='; Get-WinEvent -LogName System -MaxEvents 200 | Where-Object { $_.ProviderName -match 'Tcpip|Dhcp|DNS Client Events|Netwtw|NetBT|WLAN-AutoConfig|NlaSvc' } | Select-Object TimeCreated,ProviderName,Id,LevelDisplayName,Message -First 40 | Format-List

phases.prompt: Run read-only commands: Get-NetAdapter, Get-NetIPConfiguration, Get-DnsClientServerAddress, ipconfig /all, route print, and recent network event logs.
phases.completion: Network state and logs are captured.

phases.prompt: Analyze adapter, DNS, gateway, DHCP, proxy, VPN, and event evidence. Identify likely cause and safest repair.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Flush DNS client cache =='; Clear-DnsClientCache; Write-Output '== Renew DHCP leases =='; ipconfig /renew; Write-Output 'Targeted network repair completed. A reboot may still be required if Winsock/VPN/filter drivers are involved.'

phases.prompt: After approval/restore gate if needed, run only targeted low-risk network repair commands: DNS cache flush and DHCP renew. Do not run Winsock reset or adapter disable automatically.
phases.completion: Targeted repair completed or skipped with reason.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Adapter status =='; Get-NetAdapter | Format-Table Name,Status,LinkSpeed -AutoSize; Write-Output '== Gateway ping =='; $gw=(Get-NetIPConfiguration | Where-Object { $_.IPv4DefaultGateway } | Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty IPv4DefaultGateway).NextHop; if ($gw) { Test-Connection -ComputerName $gw -Count 2 } else { Write-Output 'No IPv4 gateway detected' }; Write-Output '== DNS resolve =='; Resolve-DnsName microsoft.com -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 5 | Format-List; Write-Output '== HTTPS reachability =='; Test-NetConnection www.microsoft.com -Port 443

phases.prompt: Verify adapter up, gateway reachable, DNS resolves, and internet endpoint reachable.
phases.completion: Connectivity is verified or remaining failure is captured.

phases.prompt: Create a technician-readable network repair report with symptom, evidence, actions/tools used, repair steps, verification result, logs/artifacts, cleanup status, residual risk, rollback notes, and follow-up.
phases.completion: User has a clear handoff report.

phases.prompt: Preserve logs and report; remove only workflow-owned temporary scratch files.

acceptance_criteria: Read-only diagnostics were captured before repair.
Risky reset commands were approved before running.
Verification includes adapter, gateway, DNS, and endpoint checks.
Report lists commands run and rollback/follow-up guidance.
